expectations.text: Pack directory exists at docs/references/modern-idiomatic-typescript/ (or similar slug)
expectations.evidence: Pack at docs/references/modern-idiomatic-typescript/ — matches skill's slug convention.

expectations.text: INDEX.md exists with frontmatter declaring mode: idioms
INDEX.md 'Version-specific warnings' section lists at least 3 training-data defaults that the pack overrides
expectations.evidence: Lists 9 training-data defaults: enum, namespace, as cast, any, class-heavy, ESLint+Prettier, type Id=string, interface-for-props-by-default, strict-only tsconfig.

expectations.text: Topic files include 'Prefer X over Y' substitutions with side-by-side do/don't code examples
expectations.evidence: satisfies-over-assertions.md has a 'Do / Don't' block with three side-by-side examples (satisfies vs variable-annotation vs as cast).

expectations.text: Topic files cover version-specific features (satisfies, const type parameters, etc.)
expectations.evidence: satisfies-over-assertions.md covers TS 4.9 satisfies; pack also has branded-types.md, no-enum-discriminated-unions.md, unknown-over-any.md.

expectations.text: Topic files explicitly cover anti-patterns (enum, namespace, any casts)
Every prescription in topic files cites a source URL
expectations.evidence: Every load-bearing rule in satisfies-over-assertions.md has an inline URL (totaltypescript.com, typescriptlang.org handbook, 2ality.com, effectivetypescript.com).

expectations.text: Pack references multiple distinct authoritative voices across topics — not resting on a single blog source
expectations.evidence: Satisfies file alone cites Matt Pocock, Axel Rauschmayer (2ality), Dan Vanderkam (Effective TypeScript). Agent report: 'Every prescription...triangulated across 2-3 independent authoritative sources.'

expectations.text: No raw/ directory — idioms mode is pure synthesis
CLAUDE.md has a 'modern idiomatic TypeScript' bullet with consult-when phrased around writing or refactoring TypeScript
expectations.evidence: Bullet: 'Consult when writing or refactoring any .ts/.tsx file, choosing lint/format tooling, or editing tsconfig.json — anchors to current practice (satisfies, discriminated unions, branded types, Biome) and overrides training-data defaults like enum, namespace, any, and ESLint+Prettier scaffolds.'
